summary: The primary characteristic is its ability to resist degradation when exposed to moisture; it maintains rigid form even under typical wetting scenarios seen clinically without losing accuracy or distorting easily upon setting. This makes options like elasticity less critical compared to stability against environmental factors such as water contact immediately post-curing stage where precision matters most.
